extraction.one_line_summary: HIRSCHI Marc waited until 50m from the Mur de Huy summit to launch his winning acceleration after WOODS Mike went at 200m.
tactical_patterns: URÁN Rigoberto's solo bridge from peloton on penultimate climb nearly succeeded, revealing value in attacking before final Mur ascent.
HIRSCHI waited until 50m to counter WOODS' 200m attack, demonstrating extreme patience pays on Mur de Huy's gradient shifts.
POLANC positioning POGAČAR at Mur base suggests UAE still treating him as leader despite post-Tour fatigue risk in autumn calendar.
Sunweb allowed HIRSCHI full freedom with no leadout, contrasting Ineos/UAE's wasted domestique efforts for non-winning leaders.

rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: positive:At 22, won with tactical maturity beyond age, holding acceleration 150m longer than Woods despite Tour stage win freshness.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: Only ninth despite team support and recent Tour victory suggests one-day climbing form doesn't transfer from three-week dominance.
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: positive: Solo bridge from peloton on penultimate climb showed tactical creativity older riders can exploit against conservative favorites.
rider_signals.WOODS Mike: negative: Going at 200m revealed he lacks explosive finish to hold off late accelerations on Mur's steepest final meters.
rider_signals.VAN SEVENANT Mauri: neutral: WorldTour debut breakaway nearly succeeded until crash; team chased him showing Deceuninck's willingness to sacrifice leader tactics.
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: negative: Sixth place despite full Ineos support indicates declining Ardennes form for former Amstel/Strade winner.
